overview: This five-color Humans deck leverages Collected Company to rapidly flood the board with low-cost creatures, applying immediate pressure while generating card advantage. Thalia's Lieutenant taxes opponent spells to slow their game plan, while Reflector Mage provides recursive value and board control by bouncing threats. The deck aims to establish an early lead, stabilize with card advantage, and close out games through combat damage or attrition.
win_conditions: Combat damage from a wide board of 1-taps and 2-drops
Card advantage loops using Reflector Mage and Collected Company
Outlasting opponents through Thalia's Lieutenant tax and efficient removal

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and a 1-drop or 2-drop
Collected Company with three or more lands
Hands containing Thalia's Lieutenant or Reflector Mage with early lands
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero or one land
Hands with only high-cost cards or removal
Hands lacking early board presence or card draw

early_game: Play lands and 1-drops to establish early board presence
Use Thalia's Lieutenant to tax opponent's early spells
Prioritize keeping hands that can play a creature by turn two
mid_game: Cast Collected Company to flood the board and gain card advantage
Use Reflector Mage to bounce key threats and generate value
Apply pressure while managing opponent's removal and board wipes
late_game: Stabilize by bouncing threats with Reflector Mage and drawing into answers
Close out games with combat damage from a wide board
Use card advantage to outvalue control decks and recover from wipes

key_cards.name: Collected Company
key_cards.notes: Cast when you have enough lands to play it and follow up with creatures; use it to recover from board wipes or establish a wide board quickly.

key_cards.name: Thalia's Lieutenant
key_cards.role: Spell tax and early board presence
key_cards.notes: Play early to increase the cost of opponent's spells; use its attack to pressure life totals while forcing opponents to spend extra mana.

key_cards.name: Reflector Mage
key_cards.notes: Bounce high-value threats or your own creatures to replay them for value; use it to disrupt opponent's board state while generating card advantage.

sequencing_tips: Cast Collected Company after playing your early creatures to maximize the number of cards drawn
Hold up mana for Reflector Mage's ability before attacking to bounce threats
Play Thalia's Lieutenant early to tax opponent's spells, but be mindful of its low power/toughness
Prioritize playing lands before casting Collected Company to ensure you can follow up
Use Reflector Mage to bounce opponent's removal or board wipes when possible

matchups.advice: Use Thalia's Lieutenant to tax early spells and buy time; prioritize keeping hands with early blockers and Collected Company to stabilize.
Apply early pressure with 1-drops and 2-drops; use Collected Company to flood the board before they stabilize; bounce their key answers with Reflector Mage.
Race them with early board presence; use Thalia's tax to slow their value engines; leverage Reflector Mage to disrupt their board state.
